summary: Records the implemented governance-text patch to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 introducing proportional tool invocation authority bands and clarifying the distinction between read-only contextual retrieval, scope-expanding retrieval, cost-bearing invocation, and high-impact execution.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Tool invocation is not a single-risk category. Read-only contextual retrieval may support accuracy, currency, provenance, and continuity where permissions are already established and no mutation, publication, cost, or external action occurs. By contrast, state-changing, public, cost-bearing, credential-bearing, deployment, generation, or irreversible actions require explicit execution-boundary scrutiny. This patch prevents both premature tool eagerness and excessive confirmation friction.

system_context.deployment_context: CAM constitutional schedule / runtime execution model
system_context.affected_population: users, maintainers, researchers, policy reviewers, and AI governance operators relying on CAM runtime governance text

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI governance / runtime governance / agentic systems

change_classification.implementation_layer: CAM constitutional schedule / runtime execution model

changed_sections: §7.4.1 Weak Trigger and Premature Tool Invocation Constraint
change_details.change_summary: The patch replaces an overly blunt tool-invocation constraint with a proportional authority model. It clarifies that auxiliary tool use must be classified by impact band before invocation and distinguishes low-impact read-only contextual retrieval from scope-expanding, private, cost-bearing, state-changing, public, irreversible, and high-impact execution.
change_details.implemented_change: Inserted §7.3.1 Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands into CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 and amended related execution-intent, weak-trigger, execution-boundary, and tool-continuity provisions.
change_details.implementation_scope: Governance-text amendment to the CAM runtime execution schedule.

implemented_changes.description: Refined execution-intent thresholding to include inspection, verification, selected connectors, available repositories, and authorised read-only contextual retrieval where non-use would materially impair accuracy, currency, provenance, continuity, or useful fulfilment.
Added authority bands distinguishing low-impact read-only contextual retrieval, scope-expanding or private retrieval, high-impact execution, and cost-bearing or quota-bearing invocation.

implemented_changes.section: §7.4.1 Weak Trigger and Premature Tool Invocation Constraint
implemented_changes.description: Clarified that mere mention of a tool-capable domain, modality, file type, repository, search pathway, image, artefact, or connector does not itself authorise tool invocation.
Updated execution-boundary handling so tool invocation must be classified under §7.3 and §7.3.1 and validated at the execution boundary where required by the impact class.
Aligned tool and artefact continuity language with the new proportional invocation model, preserving conversational continuity without independently authorising tool use.

change_details.design_rationale: The patch recognises that a system may check context without being authorised to act, and may inspect state without being authorised to mutate state. This preserves useful context acquisition while maintaining strict boundaries around mutation, publication, generation, deployment, cost, credential access, and irreversible execution.

impact_summary.primary_impact: Creates a proportional runtime governance model for auxiliary tool invocation.
impact_summary.governance_impact: Improves execution-boundary clarity by distinguishing contextual retrieval from authority-bearing execution.
impact_summary.user_impact: Reduces premature or surprising tool use while preserving useful, low-friction context checking where it is materially necessary and already within the active task envelope.
impact_summary.system_impact: Provides clearer handling for assistants, agents, repository tools, connector tools, search tools, file tools, image tools, artefact renderers, and multimodal acquisition pathways.
risk_reduction: reduces unnecessary confirmation friction for low-impact read-only retrieval
reduces accidental escalation from context checking to mutation or publication
improves provenance, currency, and execution-state clarity
known_limitations: This patch does not define every platform-specific permission model.

This patch does not remove the requirement for explicit confirmation where mutation, publication, cost, deployment, credential access, identity-bearing generation, or irreversible action is implicated.
Further UX work may be required to make authority-band transitions visible in live systems.

implementation_verification.evidence: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 was amended to include §7.3.1 Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands and related amendments to §7.3, §7.4.1, §13.8, and §20.4.4.
verification_notes: This patch note records a completed governance-text amendment rather than a proposal.
Repository validation should confirm that section references resolve correctly after insertion.
VIGIL validation should confirm that required PATCH fields are present and that CAM instruments are recorded under cam_internal rather than linked_records.standards.

items: Confirm whether the VIGIL schema supports cam_internal.changed_instruments for patch records; if not, update the schema so PATCH records use changed_instruments rather than affected_instruments.
Confirm that generated indexes correctly surface the relationship between VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 and VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002.
Review whether OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 should include a more precise subtype for proportional tool invocation, weak-trigger execution, or premature auxiliary-tool routing.

External standards, regulatory instruments, or external governance references should be recorded under linked_records.standards where applicable.
This patch should be linked from VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 as the implemented or partial repair pathway.
